sections.paragraph:
fut. O. to thrust through, to pierce, as with a sword or spear. Aram. ܕܩܰܪ, דְּקַר id. Num 25:8 Jud. 9:54 1Sa 31:4.

Niphal, pass. Isa 13:15.

Pual id. Jer 37:10 51:4 Lam 4:9, “happier are those slain by the sword than by famine, שֶׁהֵם יָזֻבוּ מְדֻּקָּרִים מִתְּנוּבֹות שָׂדַי for these waste away, pierced through, (i.e. slain) for want of the produce of the field.” מְדֻקָּרִים is put in this place by a bold figure as to those who perish from famine, as in the former member חַלְלִיִ רָעָב is opposed to חַלְלֵי חֶרֶב (comp. Isa 22:2). Vulg. contabuerunt consumti a sterilitate terrœ. Hence, בִּדְקַר, מַדְקְרֹות and-
